title: Actuation (GSM v1)
description: Structural governance marker declaring an operational intent to change a state object, derived from a sys.create/modify/delete/acquire call in a behavioral Rule body.

type.description: The kind of state mutation declared by this actuation.

stateObjectArchetype.description: Reference to StateObjectArchetype actuated by this actuation.

writtenProperties.description: Derived. Property names (dict keys) from the data argument of the sys.* call that produces this Actuation. Represents which properties of the target state object archetype are written by this actuation. Recomputed on Rule body change.

readProperties.description: Derived. Property names (dict keys) from the filter/selector argument of the sys.* call that produces this Actuation. Represents which properties are read to identify the target state object instance(s). Recomputed on Rule body change.

writtenProperties.description: Extracted from dict key names in the data argument of the sys.* call. Always derivable (keys are static even when values are dynamic). Empty array when dict construction is fully dynamic.
writtenProperties.derivedFrom: Rule body → sys.*() data argument dict keys

readProperties.description: Extracted from dict key names in the filter/selector argument of the sys.* call. Empty for CREATION (no filter). Empty array when dict construction is fully dynamic.
readProperties.derivedFrom: Rule body → sys.*() filter argument dict keys
